content:
First, note that for any fixed \(a\) and \(b\),

\[
\mathrm{lcm}(a,b)\;=\;\frac{a\,b}{\gcd(a,b)}, 
\qquad
\mathrm{lcm}(a,391)\;=\;\frac{a\cdot 391}{\gcd(a,391)}.
\]

We want
\[
\mathrm{lcm}(a,b)\;>\;\mathrm{lcm}(a,391)
\;\Longleftrightarrow\;
\frac{a\,b}{\gcd(a,b)}\;>\;\frac{a\cdot391}{\gcd(a,391)}
\;\Longleftrightarrow\;
\frac{b}{\gcd(a,b)}\;>\;\frac{391}{\gcd(a,391)}.
\]
Since \(391=17\cdot23\), the possible values of \(\gcd(a,391)\) are \(1,17,23,\) or \(391\).  But \(a<391\) rules out \(\gcd(a,391)=391\).  A quick check shows that if \(b<17\) then
\[
b\cdot23<17\cdot23=391\le 391\cdot1,
\]
so the inequality can never be satisfied.  At \(b=17\) the maximum left–side is 
\[
17\cdot23=391
\]
which only equals, but does not exceed, the minimum right–side \(391\cdot1\).

At \(b=18\) one has
\[
18\cdot 23 =414>391\cdot1,
\]
so we seek an \(a<391\) with
\[
\gcd(a,391)=23,
\qquad
\gcd(a,18)=1.
\]
For example take \(a=23\).  Then
\[
\mathrm{lcm}(23,18)=\frac{23\cdot18}{1}=414,
\qquad
\mathrm{lcm}(23,391)=\frac{23\cdot391}{23}=391,
\]
and indeed \(414>391\).  Hence \((a,b)=(23,18)\) is a “pupusa,” and one checks no smaller \(b\) can work.  

Therefore the minimum possible \(b\) is
\[
\boxed{18}.
\]
